programme.title: Energy market fallout from US-Israel strikes on Iran
programme.desc: Oil markets brace for a shock as the joint US-Israeli strikes against Iran risks disrupting the global supply of crude oil. CGTN host Lincoln Humphries talks to experts to analyze the conflict's impact on global oil supply, the repercussions of the Strait of Hormuz closure and the broader implications on international energy prices, global supply chains, and the world economy.

programme.title: Africa Live
programme.desc: Africa Live is a news program that focuses on African issues and issues related to this diverse continent. CGTN's Nairobi-based broadcast center produces Africa Live.

programme.title: Zoom in
programme.desc: A TV news magazine show packed with top stories from around the world. The weekly program zooms in on the details of trending news items and zooms out to give viewers the bigger picture. With in-depth analysis and current perspective, Zoom in takes you on a journey beyond the daily headlines.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: China 24
programme.desc: China 24 is a prime time news program featuring in-depth reports and analysis of the stories in the headlines in China.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation.

programme.title: Big Story
programme.desc: Big Story is a documentary film program on CGTN. It explores global events with in a variety of filmic styles and featuring original, in-depth, thought-provoking storytelling.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: Travelogue
programme.desc: Travelogue is a 30-minute travel program. Through the journeys of our intrepid hosts, we experience the beauty, culture and mystery of China, discovering how people live in this vast country and showcasing China's transformation to the world.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already "living in the future"!

programme.title: Inheritors
programme.desc: Inheritors is a multi-media product from CGTN that shines a light on China’s rich Intangible Cultural Heritage. Each 30-minute TV edition is hosted by a long-term expatriate in China who has a unique understanding of the country’s culture. He or she will introduce viewers to people who are dedicating their lives to its protection, inheritance, innovation, development and promotion.

programme.title: Inheritors

Each 30-minute TV edition is hosted by a long-term expatriate in China who has a unique understanding of the country’s culture. He or she will introduce viewers to people who are dedicating their lives to its protection, inheritance, innovation, development and promotion.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already "living in the future"!

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: Travelogue
programme.desc: Travelogue is a 30-minute travel program. Through the journeys of our intrepid hosts, we experience the beauty, culture and mystery of China, discovering how people live in this vast country and showcasing China's transformation to the world.

programme.title: The Vibe

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin evaluate real classics in Scottsdale, Arizona, including a Rolls-Royce Phantom II with a Merlin airplane engine.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin appraise fascinating American and European collectibles including a rare and luxurious Facel Vega FV-1 and a classic '51 Ford F1 Pickup.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 The BMW Micro Car
programme.desc: On Amelia Island, Bill Stephens and Keith Martin look under the hood to put a price on some of the finest vintage cars, including a Bel Air and Ferrari.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 American Classics - The Golden Age
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin evaluate elegant vintage automobiles including a 1932 Duesenberg from one of the country's foremost private collections.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.sub-title: S01 : E10 Two Decades of Horsepower
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin visit one of the most prestigious car shows in America and put a price on some of the most iconic cars of their time, including a Ferrari.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.sub-title: S01 : E11 The Aga Khan's Car
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin visit the Amelia Island Concours d'Elegance to appraise cars, including a rare '65 Ferrari 500 Superfast once owned by royalty.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin visit a prestigious car show and appraise vintage sports cars, including a classic Corvette and a '59 Mercedes-Benz 300SL.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin examine magnificent cars worth millions at the Gooding and Co. auction including a Best in Show winner from Pebble Beach.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 The Sports Car Craze
programme.desc: Collector car experts Bill Stephens and Keith Martin visit the annual Petersen Automotive Museum Garage Sale and Swap Meet to examine classic European sports cars.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin take a ride on the wild side when they appraise American big block beasts, including a barn find Boss 429, a Shelby GT500, and a Sting Ray.

programme.title: What's My Car Worth?
programme.desc: Bill Stephens and Keith Martin appraise muscle cars including a Plymouth Superbird and a Mustang Mach 1 at the Petersen Automotive Museum Garage Sale and Swap Meet in LA.

programme.desc: Collector car expert Bill Stephens and Keith Martin appraise some of the most iconic sports cars of all time, such as a Ferrari 365 GTB/4 Daytona and a 1965 Shelby GT350.

programme.title: Beyond the Spotlight
programme.desc: Shaquille O’Neal went from being poor and bullied – and becoming a bully himself – to fame in the NBA. The greatest influence on Shaq’s life was his step-father.

programme.title: Beyond the Spotlight
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Samuel L. Jackson & LaTanya Richardson Jackson

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: NASA's Parker Probe Plus is on a mission to learn more about the Sun -- flying through dangerous radiation to become the closest spaceship to orbit our Sun.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Opportunity: A Life on Mars
programme.desc: This year we said goodbye to one of our most intrepid planetary explorers, the Opportunity rover. Take a look back at its storied 15-year mission on Mars.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: A non-profit organization established in 2011 is aiming to land the first Israeli spacecraft on the Moon. This is the story of their attempt and the excitement of being the first private company to have a spacecraft launched with the intention of going to and landing on the moon.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Modern Day Killer Asteroid
programme.desc: 66 million years ago, an asteroid slammed into Mexico’s Yucatán Peninsula, causing a dinosaur mass extinction. What's in store for humanity if it happens again?

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: Hayabusa2 just became the first spacecraft to take subsurface samples of an asteroid. How did they do it?

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E43 Search for Our Sun's Lost Planet
programme.desc: Is it possible that there is a hidden planet within our own solar system? New evidence suggests that the fabled Planet X, or Planet 9, may indeed exist… but where?

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E53 Saturn: The Moon King
programme.desc: The latest developments in space technology continue to reveal the wonders of our solar system. With new moons recently spotted orbiting Saturn, the gas giant has taken the lead in our cosmic neighborhood's moon count.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E56 Mars Perseverance: The Hunt Begins
programme.desc: Watch as the Mars Perseverance Rover makes its historic landing on the Red Planet. We take you inside the harrowing February 18, 2021 landing and its science mission.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: This is an inside look at NASA's groundbreaking Artemis Program, the spacecraft, and the people who will bring humanity one step closer to the moon, Mars, and beyond.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E72 Capturing A Black Hole In Our Milky Way
programme.desc: Astronomers linked radio dishes around the world to create the “Event Horizon Telescope” and have utilized this tool to photograph a supermassive black hole.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.sub-title: S01 : E74 First Images: The James Webb Telescope
programme.desc: The dawn of a new era in astronomy has begun as the world gets its first look at the full capabilities of NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E23 Sailing to the Stars
programme.desc: Solar sails, the latest in space travel technology, could one day help us reach nearby stars. Follow the exciting missions laying the ground towards a new form of space exploration.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: On the 50th Anniversary of Apollo 13, Commander Jim Lovell and Apollo engineers recall the ingenuity and superhuman efforts that turned a space flight disaster into an extraordinary fight for survival.

programme.title: Pixel Collage
programme.desc: A well-crafted collage of selected short documentaries telling stories about all aspects of China! People, technology, culture, and wildlife, all are showcased in Internet-friendly form.

programme.title: Humanities and Geography
programme.desc: A strand devoted to the nature and wildlife of China, the people who lived alongside them, and the great and diverse landscapes with which they all contend.

programme.title: Living China
programme.desc: Real life stories told from the personal perspective. Moving, dramatic, sorrows and joys, expressed emphatically and expressed quietly. Watch closely to see and understand the real China as it is lived.

programme.title: Special Edition
programme.desc: Prestige Chinese documentary production, tackling serious subjects with the best cinematography, editing, and all round production values with a unique Chinese perspective.

programme.title: Journeys and Discoveries
programme.desc: A documentary series showcasing the history of China and its people.

programme.title: History in the Frame
programme.desc: A documentary series featuring historical and archaeological exploration.

programme.title: Best Eats
programme.desc: Food connects people! A feast for the eyes that spotlights the cuisines of China in a way that’s bound to set the mouths of the whole globe watering!

programme.title: Lake Geneva: The Giant Tsunami
programme.desc: In 563 AD, Geneva was destroyed by a tsunami, and it could happen again. Scientists are working to assess the danger and prepare for disaster.

programme.title: The Mystery of Dark Matter
programme.desc: Scientists studying the infinitely large and the infinitely small join forces to discover what dark matter is.

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: NASA'S 10 Greatest Achievements
programme.desc: To get a a leg up in the space race with Russia, the US declared the ultimate goal: put a man on the moon.

programme.title: NASA'S 10 Greatest Achievements
programme.desc: Apollo 8, the reusable space shuttle, the ISS, and the Apollo 13 would all put NASA one step closer.

programme.title: Asteroids: A New Horizon
programme.desc: Oumuamua, the first interstellar object detected from another star system to come this close to the Earth.

programme.title: Asteroids: A New Horizon
programme.desc: Top space resource companies all vie for competitive advantage. The new space race is just getting started.

programme.title: Battle For the Moon
programme.desc: After a string of successful space flights by the USSR, The United States responded by forming NASA.

programme.title: Battle For the Moon

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Bloody Christmas next to the Leman lake (CASE OF RUTH LÉGERET) - On January 4, 2006, Ruth Légeret was found dead in the middle of a pool of blood at her luxurious property in Vevey, Switzerland. Next to her was the lifeless body of her friend, Marina Studer. Who could have killed these two simple and wealthy widows? Clue after clue, the Swiss police's investigation would revolve around an environment of family jealousy and financial greed.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: September 16 2005. The lifeless body of Audrey Jouannet, 24, is found hidden under the bed in her small apartment in Soisy-sur-Seine. It soon becomes apparent that the young woman has been tortured at length before being strangled. But surprisingly, though the highly meticulous murderer has taken care to leave no sign of his passage, the autopsy reveals traces of sperm on the victim. It will take all of the investigators' intuition to pierce the mystery of this heinous crime...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Sylvain Dromard finds the body of his wife Laurence lying in a pool of blood in the kitchen. The crime was particularly violent and the theory of a burglary that went wrong is soon excluded. But investigators don't understand who could want the adorable hairdresser of Saint-Martin-d'Ablois dead. During the investigation, the work of profilers will enable them to draw a very precise portrait of the murderer...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: After an ordinary dinner, Pierre and Gabrielle Labrell suffer severe abdominal pains.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Nelly Crémel, 39, leaves her home to go jogging... never to return. Eight days later, after a vast search campaign, her body is found. There's no doubt that it's murder. The case soon ignites public opinion, even inflaming passions at the highest level of the state. The clues lead investigators on a number of trails, from the shadows of the French secret service to a manhunt for a possible sex maniac, in a case of murder most foul.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Willy Pomonti, a retired architect, is found savagely murdered in his opulent villa in La Celle-Saint-Cloud, near Paris. It soon becomes clear that the man has been tortured prior to death. At several points during this extraordinary enquiry, police will feel they're holding the culprit... It will take them ten years to finally shed light on this particularly violent crime.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: The lifeless body of Christophe Lejard is found by his son just metres from their home. The man was shot in the head as he was getting into his car to go to work. A veritable execution, which at first glance doesn't square with the profile of the quiet family man. Yet behind the appearances, the investigation gradually reveals all the hate and resentment that revolved around the victim.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: A man walks into a gendarmerie near Saint-Malo to report that his wife has been missing since the previous day. The couple are going through a divorce, but this mother would never have left her seven children alone! An extensive search is undertaken but the woman cannot be found. What has happened to Adeline Piet? The investigation spans several years and experiences many twists.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: The corpse of Priscillia Ciatti, 14, is discovered by the side of a field, a few hundred metres from her family home. The teenager has been stabbed sixty times with a knife and -an extra sordid detail - a devil mask is found next to her body. Was Priscillia the victim of a prowler? A serial killer? A maniac inspired by a horror movie? Investigators soon find that the young girl felt she was being watched, and even threatened...

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: In the early hours of the morning, Mathilde Croguennec, 18, attending "Teknival" in Brittany, is found at the foot of a tree with her throat cut. Investigators face the task of finding the killer from among the 43,000 participants of this huge open-air festival of electronic music, where drink and drugs are consumed in abundance. An unusual investigation that leads the police down a number of false avenues.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: Arriving at her work, the secretary of a small firm in the suburbs of Geneva finds the body of Jean-Raymond Blatti, the company's new accountant. The man is lying in a pool of blood and, on his belly, the murderer has written "DIRTY RAPIST". Yet Jean-Raymond Blatti was never charged with sexual assault. Over the course of the investigation, this inscription will appear as one of the many mysteries of this case which is particularly rich in twists and turns.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On July 19th 2004, the naked and tied up body of a woman is recovered in the Ourcq canal in Fresnes-sur-Marne. The victim is impossible to identify.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On April 3th 2003, Chantal Ternik, a 48 year old housewife, is discovered hanged in her house of Sanary-sur-Mer, in Var.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On March 28th 2013, the burnt body of Anne Barbot, 38 years old, is discovered in a forest of Maine-et-Loire.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On May 17th 2005, a body is discovered in a forest of Sucy-en-Brie; Christophe Belle, 40 years old, a renowned pastry cook.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: The disturbing missing case in Paris of a four year old.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On October 13th 1996, the body of young Angélique Dumetz, 18 years old, is recovered down a ditch in Oise forest. An important clue is found on the crime scene.

programme.title: Crime Scene Solvers
programme.desc: On February 23th 2017, policemen are alerted of the disapearrance of Brigitte Troadec, her husband Pascal, and their children Sébastien and Charlotte.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a talented television news anchor is shot to death outside her home, it appears to be a crime of passion perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An employee of a dry cleaner was raped and murdered in the store. Police turned to forensic science for the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police were puzzled by an obscure print found at a crime scene where one man had been killed and two teenage girls were seriously injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a trio of unsolved murders occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas. Later, a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with far more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a mini-vacation in Key West. The wife went missing, and police searched every square inch of the island, they found nothing but a pair of sandals which might have belonged to her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Prompted by news of a flight attendant's brutal murder, the son of a previous victim contacts police.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman was killed instantly when a bomb exploded in her home. The device was so powerful that shrapnel was embedded in houses across the street. The bomber had not only knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A patrolman was dispatched to what he thought would be a routine traffic call until he looked in the car.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter is the victim of a well-planned crime. Investigators suspect two weapons and two killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist starts several fires that cause millions of dollars worth of damage and the deaths of three people. He is discovered, partly through the use of forensic hypnosis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An Alaskan police officer finds a woman's body while on routine patrol of a public park. During the autopsy, the medical examiner preserves the portion of her rib cage which sustained the fatal wound.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A union official is executed in his home not long after a strike by the union membership. Neither his wife nor anyone in the neighborhood heard any gunshots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bombings are difficult to solve, because the perpetrator isn't usually at the scene, and the evidence goes up in smoke. In this case pieces of plastic the size of grains of sand hold the key to a man's murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A cop is found dead. His wife first says the motive was robbery; then she tells police it was suicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the state of Florida was known for more than just Disney World. Worldwide attention focused on a rash of robberies which targeted tourists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A fire erupted in Kings Cross Station, killing 31 people and injuring dozens more. Investigators pinpointed the cause of the fire with state-of-the-art technology.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Alaskan hikers discover a body wrapped in sheets which were edged in orange stitching. Investigators find a tuft of red carpet fibers clinging to the sheet - threads of evidence which lead them straight to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Native American woman was brutally killed in the desert of New Mexico, and the crime scene was rich in forensic evidence: tire tracks, shoe impressions and even the murder weapons.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared along a California highway in 1991. Three years later, the case was solved when a man's refrigerated truck was looked upon with suspicion.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1987 disappearance of Houston resident Tracy Jo Shine is recalled. The investigation went cold until 2000 when a "cold case squad" learned that the woman's ex-boyfriend Michael Neal had bragged about killing her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15-year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace. Even though foul play is suspected, the leads don't pan out and the trail turns cold. Twenty years would pass before police are able to link a clump of hair discovered in an isolated area with the missing woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, the founder of American Atheists disappeared with one of her sons, her granddaughter and $500,000 in gold coins. The police investigated several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect became ill and died just before she was to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy revealed she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators had to determine which person who knew her had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Emergency dispatch received a call from a man who said his girlfriend shot and killed herself. The autopsy revealed that the gunshot wound was not self-inflicted and the evidence on her body would give police a golden opportunity to catch her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man riding a bicycle was fatally injured, and police believed he was the victim of a hit-and-run accident. Tiny clues found at the scene created a picture of the vehicle which struck him and led police to its driver.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car, the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Dropped S4 - "Bearing Down"

programme.desc: When Wardens respond to a call sometimes getting there is most of the battle. And we pay tribute to an honorary warden that will truly be missed.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "Stolen Future"
programme.desc: Montana Game Wardens have their hands full with headless deer, trophy bull elk being poached and illegally tagged deer. But the Future of hunting is bright with a new batch of hunters.

programme.title: South Seas Spearo S2 - "North Minerva Reef"
programme.desc: After punching their way to North Minerva, the fellas continue their quest to land a big pelagic, but the local toothies threaten to throw a spanner into the team’s plans. Can they bag a tropical trophy, or will the sharks rain on their parade?

programme.title: South Seas Spearo S2 - "Yellowfin Mission"
programme.desc: Word's gotten out about some massive yellowfin baitballs, so the team head out in search of more tuna glory. Dwane, Kieran, Sam and Dave then bounce over the ditch for a few days diving the stunning waters of the Great Barrier Reef.

programme.title: South Seas Spearo S2 - "Lucinda Sky with Diamonds"
programme.sub-title: S02 : E11 Lucinda Sky with Diamonds
programme.desc: The lads try their luck at getting a spear into one of the quintessential Australian fish species - the barramundi. Problem is, these 'barra' inhabit the same waters as a couple of other less welcome locals - saltwater crocodiles and bull sharks!

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Nightmare Canyon - Part 1"
programme.desc: Justin and Jeremy Harris’ weekend decent into a remote Utah canyon becomes a survival nightmare when Justin severely breaks his leg. As night fast approaches and temperatures drop below freezing, how will these two brothers survive?

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Nightmare Canyon - Part 2"
programme.desc: After Justin Harris severely breaks his leg deep in a remote, Utah canyon, it ‘s up to his brother Jeremy to climb out for help. Clawing his way through icy pools of water and up rock faces, pushes Jeremy to his limit.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Under The Desert Sun"
programme.sub-title: S03 : E03 Under The Desert Sun
programme.desc: Ed Rosenthal set out for a short hike in Joshua Tree National Park, but in the heat of the day he became turned around and lost. His six-day ordeal of survival in blazing temperatures with no food or water is nothing short of miraculous.

programme.title: Backcountry Rescue S1 - "Episode 4"
programme.desc: The team battles high winds in the heli to save a snowmobiler with a busted femur.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S17 - "Bayview Outfitters"
programme.desc: Colin McKeown and friend Howard Sonnenburg are in Newfoundland chasing Atlantic Salmon. It's Howard's first experience with Salmon!

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S17 - "Regions of Ontario"
programme.desc: Highlighting some of the finest fishing locations in Northern Ontario for multi species.

programme.title: Bottom Feeders S1 - "FAMILY TIES"
programme.desc: It's late spring in the upper midwest and our commercial fishermen continue to endure a difficult season. The unpredictable and erratic weather conditions are an opportunity for crews to pull together.

programme.title: Bottom Feeders S1 - "SUMMER"
programme.desc: This time of year is usually a time for commercial fishermen to think about vacation but due to a poor spring, Mike, Tim, Jedd and their crews are fishing well into summer.

programme.title: Bottom Feeders S1 - "NEW BEGINNINGS IN AUTUMN"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 NEW BEGINNINGS IN AUTUMN
programme.desc: As the season changes from summer to fall, change is also in store for our commercial fishermen. One man is just starting out, one is barely getting by, and one is looking to start over.

programme.title: Dropped S4 - "High Waters"
programme.desc: Stuck in the tent for days, Chris and Kaid finally get a break in the weather. They realize the rain has flooded the river dangerously. Nonetheless, the hunt for big game persists.

programme.title: Dropped S4 - "Moose Fever"
programme.desc: Chris and Kaid pursue a big bull moose, and Kaid finds himself with hide in his crosshairs. Casey and R.L. continue their search for big game. The high river waters refuse to quit raging.

programme.title: Dropped S4 - "Weary Travelers"
programme.desc: R.L. and Casey venture far from camp to access prime moose territory during the rut. Meanwhile, bad weather prompts Chris to scout a potential new extraction point.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "Night Chase"
programme.desc: With a massive Winter storm bearing down on Northern Montana Wardens aren't backing off. Wardens catch one guy in a web of lies at a check station while miles away they chase down a decoy shooter that refuses to give up.

programme.title: South Seas Spearo S2 - "Just Another Fijian"
programme.desc: Dwane and Jools are joined by their pint sized proteges this week, as 12-year-olds Eli and Reef tag along on the adventure with the old men on a mission to explore the far flung waters of North Eastern Fiji.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Tree Stand Nightmare"
programme.desc: When avid bow hunter, Dr. Joe Baumgardner sets out to do routine maintenance on his tree stands, a twenty two foot fall leaves him with a severely broken leg and miles from help in the deep Mississippi woods.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Crushed By A Boulder - Part 1"
programme.sub-title: S03 : E05 Crushed By A Boulder - Part 1
programme.desc: While solo climbing Mt. Goddard, a remote peak in the California Sierra, Gregg Hein’s leg is crushed by a boulder and he’s sent careening down the mountain. Barely able to stop his fall, he’s now faced with a horrific compound fracture.

programme.title: Backcountry Rescue S1 - "Episode 5"
programme.desc: Unprecedented three rescues in under twelve hours.

programme.title: Backcountry Rescue S1 - "Episode 6"
programme.desc: Jamie puts his trust in new explosives technology.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Bahamas"
programme.desc: Mark Melnyk travels to Andros Island to fly fishing at 4 different lodges to experience the ultimate bonefish trip!

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Teton Valley Lodge (Idaho)"
programme.desc: Mark Melnyk is in beautiful Yellowstone Teton Territory fly fishing for rainbow trout and cutthroat. He is the guest of Teton Valley Lodge

programme.title: Madfin Shark Series S1 - "Day Three Begins"
programme.desc: Day three ends at Hurricane Hole Marina in Key West, Florida, on the Madfin Shark Series & there can bo only one Madfin Shark Champion.

programme.title: Dropped S4 - "Lifted"
programme.desc: As their Alaska journey nears its end, Kaid and R.L. reflect on life's priorities and lessons learned. Lifelong bonds are cemented as they prepare for extraction.

programme.title: Dropped S5 - "Empty Handed"
programme.desc: The Keefer brothers put boots to ground as their journey through Alaska begins. Armed with nothing but a couple Glock 20s, the brothers immediately begin their search for their first supply-yielding cache.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "Wasted Dummy"
programme.desc: This week Warden Lesofski searches out a hunting party that left their elk to rot. While Region 2 Wardens rush into a volatile situation after their decoy is shot from the road.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "Minor Mistakes"
programme.desc: A riverside check station catches four minors with no life jackets and a lot of alcohol. Then Warden Golie stops two car full of minors that should not be behind the wheel.

programme.title: Bottom Feeders S1 - "WORLDS COLLIDE"
programme.desc: The rough season is starting to wear on commercial fishermen. For some, hard feelings are boiling to the surface and others turn imagination into money.

programme.title: Bottom Feeders S1 - "FISHING IS NOT FAIR"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E10 FISHING IS NOT FAIR
programme.desc: The desire and competition for catching fish is at an all time high but fishing is anything but fair.

programme.title: Bottom Feeders S1 - "STORM IS BREWING"
programme.desc: A distant natural disaster creates uncertainty for all commercial fishing crews in the upper Midwest. Jedd finds a valuable species in his haul but isn’t sure a market exists. Tim races against shifting conditions in hopes of scoring a decent haul.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Crushed By A Boulder - Part 2"
programme.sub-title: S03 : E06 Crushed By A Boulder - Part 2
programme.desc: After his leg is crushed by a boulder, Gregg Hein is stranded on a high Sierra peak with an infected compound fracture. Knowing he won’t be reported missing for days, Gregg attempts to crawl off the mountain and rescue himself.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Iraqi Sniper Attack"
programme.desc: On deployment in Anbar Province, Marine Corp Master Sergeant Buck Doyle throws himself in harm's way to save a fallen comrade from an Iraqi sniper and becomes a target himself. This is a story of survival on and off the battlefield.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Lightning Strike"
programme.desc: A bass fishing trip goes horribly wrong when a bolt of lightning strikes their boat, leaving Seventeen-year-old Christian Neal burned and unconscious and his father Gabe blown into the water and paralyzed. It will take a miracle to save them.

programme.title: Backcountry Rescue S1 - "Episode 7"
programme.desc: A young snowmobiler's first ride into the backcountry spins out of control.

programme.title: Backcountry Rescue S1 - "Episode 8"
programme.desc: Tim demands a lot of everyone around him, especially during a cave rescue - what he describes as "the worst kind of rescue possible".

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Waterfalls Lodge (Algoma)"
programme.desc: Jenna McKeown is on the hunt for big smallmouth bass on topwater flies in Northern Ontario. She is in luck as a major dragonfly hatch is about to happen.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Flowers River Lodge (Labrador)"
programme.desc: Labrador is renowned for big Brook Trout but also Atlantic Salmon. Host Colin McKeown visits one of the top salmon lodges in the world.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Ugly Bug Fly Shop (Wyoming)"
programme.sub-title: S16 : E05 Ugly Bug Fly Shop (Wyoming)
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Surviving a Mountain Lion Attack"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Surviving a Mountain Lion Attack
programme.desc: Laura and Nick explore the techniques to deal with these apex predators when they are the most dangerous. We’ll scientifically breakdown the behaviors, strength, eyesight and speed of mountain lions through outrageous experiments, in day and night.

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "LOST!"
programme.desc: Nick and Laura head deep into the mountains to explore survival techniques that go far beyond anything they’ve learned before. Our courageous hosts subject themselves to intense survival training with the Survival Training School of California.

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "ATV Accidents"
programme.desc: ATVs have allowed us to penetrate the wilderness like never before. But they’ve also made the backcountry a bit more dangerous. Laura and Nick rig up some dramatic, high-speed tests to see how accidents happen and what you can do to protect yourself.

programme.title: Police Patrol S1 - "TOWNSHIP POLICE PATROLS"
programme.desc: Rio de Janeiro’s beaches draw tourists, but beyond the picture postcard imagery lies a harsher reality—favelas ruled by drug gangs. To reclaim these areas, the government deployed BOPE, an elite police force taking the fight to the streets since 2008.

programme.title: Police Patrol S1 - "NARCOTRAFFIC POLICE PATROLS"
programme.desc: As one of the world’s top cocaine producers, Peru is a magnet for global drug cartels. To combat the trade at its source, the elite anti-narcotics unit Sinchi was formed—taking the fight deep into the heart of production zones.

programme.title: Police Patrol S1 - "TOURIST POLICE PATROLS"
programme.desc: With one of the highest homicide rates in the world, Acapulco sees 104 murders per 100,000 people. In this deadly environment, a special anti-crime unit puts their lives on the line daily to keep the city from descending into chaos.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "True Lies"
programme.desc: A check station outside of Billings stops a guy who refuses to tell the truth. Investigating who shot the deer reveals a long history of game violations.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "Head Case"
programme.desc: Someone shot and stashed the head of a trophy bull elk. Wardens spend months staking out the head. Once it is moved they use high tech equipment to locate the head and capture the suspect.

programme.title: Wardens S5 - "Blood Trail"
programme.desc: Wardens step into the world of CSI this week in determining where a deer was killed. But when uncovering the truth the best thing is to follow the Blood Trail.

programme.title: Dropped S5 - "Long Days, Cold Nights"
programme.sub-title: S05 : E02 Long Days, Cold Nights
programme.desc: The Keefer brothers awaken to a frozen tundra. With over 8 miles to cover and no oars, the brothers are forced to trudge their way through shallow water and barren earth as they head downstream in search of they second cache.

programme.title: Dropped S5 - "Choke Point"
programme.desc: The terrain begins to change as the Keefer brothers are forced to pick their way downstream. The landscape tightens it’s grip and funnels the brothers to a geographic choke point where both predator and prey gather.

programme.title: Dropped S5 - "Bone-Weary"
programme.desc: The Keefer brothers take shelter in a small pine outcropping as a winter storm sets siege to the river. Forced to wait out the arctic blast, the brothers try and retain what little energy they have left.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Mountain Lion Attack"
programme.desc: Marine Anne Hjelle was mountain biking when she was ripped off her bike by a mountain lion. Anne fought back even as the big cat shredded her face in its jaws. Surviving the attack was only the beginning of Anne’s incredible fight to survive.

programme.title: Fight to Survive S3 - "Soul Of A Survivor"
programme.sub-title: S03 : E10 Soul Of A Survivor
programme.desc: Craig DeMartino examines the stories of other survivors he’s met across the season. Their survival stories are all different, but Craig discovers that these survivors share more in common than you might think.

programme.title: The Trail S1 - "Into The High Lonesome"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Into The High Lonesome
programme.desc: Modern day Mountain Man Laramy Miller sets out on the trail to experience life in the high lonesome.

programme.title: The Trail S1 - "Camp Otis"
programme.desc: Life at Camp Otis. Laramy finds success fishing and foraging, and spends his time taking care of 7 and doing camp chores.

programme.title: The Trail S1 - "Relocation"
programme.desc: Laramy realizes that game is scarce because of the wolves and he decides to put some distance between himself and the pack. Time to break down camp and move to a new location.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Calmwaters Fly Fishing (Ontario)"
programme.desc: Bill Spicer explores the rivers of southern Ontario with several guides to catch brown and rainbow trout.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Big Horn Angler Fly Shop (Montana)"
programme.sub-title: S16 : E07 Big Horn Angler Fly Shop (Montana)
programme.desc: Mark Melnyk gets the opportunity to take a drift boat trip down the famed Big Horn River in Montana searching for trout on a fly.

programme.title: The New Fly Fisher S16 - "Fish Tales Fly Shop (Calgary Alberta)"
programme.sub-title: S16 : E08 Fish Tales Fly Shop (Calgary Alberta)
programme.desc: Jenna McKeown visits Calgary Alberta to fish the Bow River. This is one of the few rivers in the world that runs through a major city yet has excellent fishing.

programme.title: South Seas Spearo S2 - "Three Kings Redemption"
programme.desc: After a break of dawn beach launch off the tip of 90 Mile Beach, the team punch north to the legendary Three Kings Islands, home to dozens of record calibre fish, and in the process, bag a couple of new records for themselves.

programme.title: South Seas Spearo S2 - "The Rock"
programme.desc: The Spearo team are in the deep South this week, departing Bluff for a couple of days diving the pristine waters of Stewart Island, but will the brief weather window allow them to make the notorious steam all the way to remote Solander Island?

programme.desc: Miehistö irrottaa jadelohkareita J-linssistä, mutta iso ongelma tiellä uhkaa kaikkea.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kun Claudia ja Robini etsivät jadea poraamalla tuloksetta, edes yhtiön geologit eivät pääse yksimielisyyteen suunnitelmasta.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Claudia, Robin ja miehistö yrittävät saada raskaan kaluston vuorelle ennen kuin tulvivat joet katkaisevat pääsyn kaivosalueelle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Ilmassa on jännitettä, kun miehistö yrittää saada raskasta kalustoa mutaiselle, lohkareiden peittämälle 120 kilometrin pituiselle polulle perustaakseen uuden jadekaivoksen vuorenhuipulle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Miehistö yrittää saada koneensa kulkemaan vyöryvien jokien yli perustaakseen uuden jadekaivoksensa vuorenhuipulle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Alan on vakuuttunut siitä, että Dynastyn alueella veden täyttämän kuopan alla on jadea.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Robin yrittää uhmata fysiikan lakeja saadakseen massiivisen uuden kasarmin Wolverinen alueelle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Jade Cityn porukkaa koettelee huono onni, ja jokainen keksii ainutlaatuisia ratkaisuja huonon onnen karkottamiseksi.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kaikki innostuvat, kun he vihdoin alkavat kaivaa esiin lupaavia vihreitä lohkareita, joiden miehistö uskoo olevan miljoonien arvoisia.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Jännitteet nousevat pintaan, kun ulkomaiset sijoittajat piipahtavat yllättäen tarkistamaan Wolverinen kaivosalueen töitä, ja Alan yrittää rauhoittaa tilannetta.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kun Wolverinen kaivoksella vihdoin löydetään lupaavia lohkareita, miehistö huomaa, että he ovat saattaneet mennä tonttinsa rajojen ulkopuolelle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Jännitys kasvaa, kun kaivoskauden loppu lähestyy, ja Jade Cityn miehistö haluaa löytää kallisarvoisen lohkareen.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Jade Cityn kaivostyöläiset yrittävät löytää rikkauksia - tarpeeksi suuren jadelohkareen, joka tekee vaikutuksen sijoittajiin - ennen kauden päättymistä.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Claudian ja Robinin testaamattomat koneet alkavat hajota raa'alla matkalla Wolverinen kaivosalueelle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Miehistö yrittää ylittää tulvivia jokia matkalla kaivokselle koneet mukanaan, mutta Josh jää jälkeen ongelmallisen kaivinkoneen takia.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Josh jää jumiin pitkällä matkalla Wolverinen kaivokselle, kun pultit irtoavat kaivinkoneesta eikä hän voi kutsua apua.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kaivoskausi alkaa, ja vaikka jadea ei näy, rekkaonnettomuus tuo Jade Citylle onnea.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Guysta tulee Wolverinen johtaja, ja uuden työntekijän palkkaaminen uhkaa suistaa hänen tehtävänsä löytää kauden ensimmäinen jadelohkare.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Juuri kun Wolverinen miehistö tehostaa turvallisuusmenetelmiään, uusi työntekijä Jay romahtaa, mikä paljastaa heidän hätävalmiutensa säröt.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Josh käyttää uutta kaivinkonetta, kun taas arvottomien kivien kasa saa Claudian harkitsemaan uutta strategiaa.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Alan tuo Jade Cityyn ostajaryhmän, mikä saa Claudian tiimin paniikkiin ja etsimään kauden ensimmäiset myytävät jadelohkareet.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kun miehistö rämpii mudassa etsiessään miljoonan dollarin arvoista lohkaretta, Josh riskeeraa puolen miljoonan dollarin arvoisen kaluston yrittäessään vetää rekkaansa pois vajoamasta.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Tiimi löytää kauden parhaan jadelohkareen, mutta on vaarassa heikentää sen arvoa, kun he yrittävät leikata kaikki halkeamat pois.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Claudian poika Justin osoittaa, että hän osaa löytää jadea, kun taas Scrappy Larry, Robin ja Josh kamppailevat hinaamaan rikkinäisen autonsa Wolverinelle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Huonot tulokset pakottavat Robinin ja Justinin viimeiseen yritykseen etsiä jadekiviä pelastaakseen kauden, kun taas Scrappy Larry kiirehtii muuntamaan dieselillä toimivan säiliön.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Claudian hanke majoittaa työryhmä Jade Cityyn vaarantuu, kun saostussäiliö vuotaa juomaveteen.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kylmän sään saapuessa aikaisin ja velkojen kasaantuessa miehistö työntää, vetää ja raahaa mammuttimaisen ja mahdollisesti kauden pelastavan lohkareen leirille.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kun vuoden lupaavinta lohkaretta työstetään Wolverinella, Josh ja R2 kolaroivat rekka-autonsa, ja Claudia tiimineen jäävät ymmälleen.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Kilpailu on käynnissä, jotta Wolverinen leiristä päästään pois ennen kuin kaikki jää lumen alle.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI
programme.desc: Claudia joutuu ajamaan kiviautoa, kun hänen miehistönsä kamppailee kivistä polkua ylös Wolverinen vuorenhuipulla sijaitsevaan leiriin.

programme.title: Jade Fever | FI

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon sets out to transform people's living spaces. Before moving in to their 1940s ex-council house in Co Kilkenny, the Kirwans added a basic extension.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot wants to turn the house completely on its head, putting the bedrooms to the front and extending the back to include a kitchen, living and dining that leads into the garden.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot is in Templetuohy, Co. Tipperary with Joanne and Sean Cleere who believe they need a substantial extension but Dermot doesn't actually concur.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Room to Improve starts with Margaret and Jodie Burke who plan to renovate the family home in Foxrock.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Pádraig Whooley and Lorraine Dufficey spent 265 thousand euros on their remote cottage at Rossmore in West Cork, they knew that - while the hilltop location was ideal - the house was far from it.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot is in Laytown, co.Meath, with a budget of 60,000 euros, Dermot's worry "is even if you took away the shoddy workmanship you'd spend the 60k, this is a really difficult one..."

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot is in Dalkey, with a budget of 180,000 euros, he must come up with an innovative design to match their exacting requirements within the confines of an incredibly tight site.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot is in Clontarf, with Denise and Ben who desperately want a second bathroom for their family home, but their budget is limited.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot is in Tullow, with Tom and Lisa who want to renovate their 19th century farmhouse.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Yvonne and John live in a housing estate in Dundalk with their two children. Four years ago, they decided to buy their next-door neighbours house.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Jim and Siobhan Doyle live in a beautiful old house over looking Dublin Bay in Sutton. See Dermot Bannon transform the rambling old house into a beautiful new space.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: The series begins in Cork City with our youngest and most ambitious couple yet, Meadhbh and Niall, both 27-years-old.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: After 10 successful years living in Boston, Elaine Mitchell made the decision to come back to the old sod with her earnings.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: No close neighbours, beautiful views of the Wicklow Mountains, a large vegetable patch, 10 hens, four ducks, two geese, four beehives, two dogs and three cats.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Marcus and Margaret are about to extend a two-bed cottage in Dundrum and share it with Marcus's beloved Aunt May.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: The number one criteria when buying a house is location and our fifth programme of the series is set in one of the most sought after costal parts of the country, Dalkey.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: John Rochford from Dublin and Lee Benn, originally from Limerick, have bought a house in their dream location - South Dublin's seaside suburbia, Blackrock.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot Bannon helps a family to transform their 70s bungalow outside Tipperary into a modern family home.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: This week Dermot goes to Cork to work with a family spanning three generations - who all have different ideas on how to improve their home.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot Bannon tries to turn a dilapidated, woodworm infested 1930s cottage into a starter home for a Finglas family.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: This week Dermot tackles a large Victorian terraced house in the highly sought-after Dublin suburb of Donnybrook.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: The first in the season will see Catherine Bennett and her youngest daughter Sarah Jane keen to downsize from their huge 13-bedroomed house in Mullingar. They're planning to move down the road, where they also own a 3-bed bungalow that's been uninhabited for the last nine years.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot faces a unique challenge in Clane, Co. Kildare.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon helps Senator Katherine Zappone and Dr Ann Louise Gilligan renovate a dilapidated hunting lodge in Brittas, Dublin.

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Desperate for more space, a couple with two young daughters, who have long since outgrown their tiny cottage in Killawalla outside Westport, engage Dermot to help.

programme.desc: Can two expats cope with their customer complaints? After a blocked drain threatened their future, have the owners of a Spanish seafood restaurant turned things around?

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Shanghai"

programme.title: House Hunt S1 - "A Seller's Market"

programme.title: House Hunt S1 - "Rollercoaster Ride"

programme.title: House Hunt S1 - "The City and the Country"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 The City and the Country
programme.desc: City boy Ethan Tey gears up for an Auckland auction and in Blenheim will Joanna and Chris stop fighting long enough to buy their first home?

programme.title: Journeys in India S1 - "India’s Natural Beauties: Udaipur and Jaipur"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 India’s Natural Beauties: Udaipur and Jaipur
programme.desc: While traveling by boat, we visit the many palaces and temples on the shore. We also tour the majestic City Palace and the Garden of the Maids of Honor. In Jaipur, we tour the Pink City, seeing treasures as the Palace of Winds and the Amber Fort.

programme.title: Journeys in India S1 - "Hyderabad: The Lake City"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Hyderabad: The Lake City
programme.desc: Join us as we discover the amazing history of this ancient city through its religion, royal lineage, architecture and art. In the markets, Bill shows you how to buy souvenirs like a pro.

programme.title: Journeys in India S1 - "The Wildlife of Rajastan: Ranthambore & Keoladeo National Parks"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 The Wildlife of Rajastan: Ranthambore & Keoladeo National Parks
programme.desc: Ranthambore National Park is part of Project Tiger, a unique wildlife conservation program set up to insure the preservation of the magnificent Tiger. In Ranthambore we encounter the tiger along with Sambar and Blue Bull, Wild Boar and Langur Monkey.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "We Bought a Resort (Part 2)"
programme.sub-title: S02 : E26 We Bought a Resort (Part 2)
programme.desc: Each with a big personality of their own, it becomes clear this couple is not going to see eye to eye on what they want. Luckily for them, Sofie is a pro at mediating conflicts when it comes to real estate.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S3 - "Net Zero"
programme.desc: Julie and James are looking for a new home to raise their two boys. James is in the tech industry and would love an energy efficient home. Julie has her own dance studio and can't wait to get back to work after giving birth to her second son.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S3 - "Wow Factor"
programme.desc: Giulia and Scott have recently come into an inheritance and want the house of their dreams.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Ulagala, Sri Lanka - Florence"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 Ulagala, Sri Lanka - Florence
programme.desc: From Michael Angelo’s David in Florence to elephant safaris in Sri Lanka, this is an art lovers and outdoorsy adventure.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Algarve & Parrot Cay (Turks & Caicos Islands)"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Algarve & Parrot Cay (Turks & Caicos Islands)
programme.desc: From miles of soft golden sand in Portugal’s Algarve region, to the miles of sand that surrounds Parrot Cay Island in the Turks and Caicos, this is a beach lover’s episode.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Barcelona & London"
programme.desc: If you’re a budding architect or simply an admirer of beautiful buildings this episode takes in some of best in Europe from London and Barcelona.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "France"
programme.desc: A pool leak threatens plans at a Spanish holiday complex. A French family questions their renovation challenge and new Spanish bar owners prepare for tourist season.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "French Renovation"
programme.desc: How will Spain’s latest hoteliers deal with the feedback from their guests? After tiresome work, a French renovation project hits crisis point and a family moves to the Costa del Sol to open their dream bar.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Review Rankings"
programme.desc: A family faces eviction hours after arriving in Spain. In France, a couple seeks creative funding for their dream and the owners of a Spanish bar embark on a mission to climb the review rankings.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Maitland"
programme.desc: Alana stumbles upon the secret of eternal youth and Scotty goes under lock and key in Maitland

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Borneo"
programme.desc: Scotty gets in touch with his ancestral roots and David get's a new friend in Borneo.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S4 - "South Africa, Fiji"
programme.desc: Scotty is in beautiful South Africa, searching for the Big 5. Carolyne explores the wonderful ancient city of Rome. Lynton heads to the hills of Fiji and is rewarded with an amazing surprise. And Susannah is in the Big Apple, New York.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "CYPRUS & IBIZA"
programme.desc: Mediterranean, Mythology, and Fusion - These are just a few things that Cyprus can offer! Ibiza is undoubtedly one of the most stunning Islands in the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "JOHANNESBURG & BOTSWANA"
programme.desc: In Johannesburg our crew stayed in style in the luxurious Westcliff Hotel. In Botswana, we visit three remarkable Safari camps.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "GOA & SINGAPORE"
programme.desc: In Goa, the team look at the incredible wildlife and beaches of this former Portuguese Colony. Staying in Asia our team discover the presidential suite in Fullerton – Singapore.

programme.title: Khanh Ong's Wild Food S1 - "Mallacoota, VIC"
programme.desc: Chef Khanh Ong Travels to Mallacoota, Victoria to explore the seafood delicacies that the area has to offer but decides to make a few pitstops along the way. He learns how to make blue cheese and picks out some wine to match with seafood dishes.

programme.title: Khanh Ong's Wild Food S1 - "Tasmania - Part 1"
programme.desc: Chef Khanh Ong takes a ride on the Spirit of Tasmania to explore Northern Tasmania and tries hunting of a different kind, gets is hands dirty harvesting a local crop and gets an insight into cooking with a protein only available in Tasmania.

programme.title: The Delinquent Gourmet S1 - "Fez, Morocco"
programme.desc: In Fez, Morocco, not much has changed since the Middle Ages but Rodney is determined to stir things up and find tantalizing new dishes he can bring home.

programme.title: The Delinquent Gourmet S1 - "Sardinia"
programme.desc: Rodney is in Sardinia, Italy, where he finds culinary inspiration around every corner. High in the Sardinian hills, his hosts prepare their specialty - roasted suckling pig. After sitting down to an outdoor family meal (where even the dog helps himself!), Rodney samples a myrtle-flavored liquor before dancing with his gentlemen hosts.

programme.title: Chasing Monsters: El Nino S1 - "Family Values"
programme.desc: A new generation of female chargers band together to compete in a unique big wave surfing ‘expression session’ on the north shore of Oahu.

programme.title: Chasing Monsters: El Nino S1 - "Skullbase"
programme.desc: The Walsh twins reveal why water safety crews and rescue teams are the unsung heroes of big wave surfing.

programme.title: Streetfood Nomad: Kolkata S1 - "The Street Scene"
programme.desc: Kolkata’s extraordinary street-food scene has been influenced by its various conquerors and rulers. The most profound influence has been from the fabulously rich and powerful Mughals. Will reimagines classic dishes while also incorporating the unique street food flavours he encounters.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "The Honeymoon is Over"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E13 The Honeymoon is Over
programme.desc: Tayler and Jason take on their final peak in Washington State, Mt Baker. Tayler struggles when Jason explains all the things that could go wrong, but the reality is a lot could fail if they don't come together as a team.

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Tricks S1 - "Delhi & Mumbai"
programme.desc: Pete is in Delhi and Mumbai performing magic to a variety of people, including a baffling thirst quenching trick, as well as making rings and himself vanish!

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Tricks S1 - "Manchester"
programme.desc: Pete Heat checks out some of the trendiest bars in the Mumbai, meets a famous Bollywood artist and shows you how to perform your own piece of magic at home.

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Tricks S1 - "Mumbai"
programme.desc: Pete Heat in Manchester and visits Old Trafford and for his finale; he wows a group of friends with his unique and comedic magic skills.

programme.title: 7 Days S1 - "Day 4"
programme.desc: Jalou and Kevin travel to far colder places to challenge each other in an ice climbing competition.

programme.title: 7 Days S1 - "Day 5"
programme.desc: In the Chinese desert, Kevin finds out that desert racing is difficult. While in Bolivia, Jalou wants to prove to her brother that women can be better drivers.

programme.title: 7 Days S1 - "Day 6"
programme.desc: After exploring Peru, Jalou has to get over her fear when she goes white water kayaking. In Thailand, Kevin is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Sahara"
programme.desc: Francesca is in Morocco to experience life on the edge of human capability and takes on a survival challenge walking alone amongst the sand dunes of the Sahara.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "New Orleans"
programme.desc: Francesca Chiorando travels to New Orleans to explore Voodoo and learn about the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Massachusetts"
programme.desc: Francesca is in the USA, in an area named The Bridgewater Triangle to discover if there’s any truth to its claim as the most paranormal area of the USA.

programme.title: Epic Trails S1 - "Winter in Banff & Lake Louise"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Winter in Banff & Lake Louise
programme.desc: Eric Hanson returns to Banff National Park and Lake Louise in the snowy months to explore the backcountry by snowshoe and snowboard.

programme.title: Epic Trails S1 - "The Jordan Trail"
programme.desc: Eric Hanson heads to Jordan to explore the newly developed ‘Jordan Trail’—a 450-mile hiking trail that crosses the country and passes through some of the world’s most inspiring landscapes and historical sites, including the ancient city of Petra.

programme.title: Epic Trails S2 - "Papua New Guinea"
programme.desc: Eric Hanson visits wild and remote villages in Papua New Guinea, as he challenges himself on one of the world’s most difficult trails—the Kokoda Track.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "North Pole"
programme.desc: The crew takes an icebreaker to the North Pole just in time to make a satellite phone call to wish Bill's mother a happy birthday (she is so shocked she doesn't recognize him). We also track and film the biggest predators on earth…polar bears.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "Rwanda…Gorillas"
programme.desc: Bill tracks through never ending mud, heavy vegetation and steep mountains, to come face-to-face with our mighty cousins. He also traces the life, and finds the remnants, of the camp of one of his idols - Diane Fossey.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "Botswana"
programme.desc: Botswana is a wildlife lover’s dream with massive herds of elephants, immense pods of hippos and some of the largest prides of lions anywhere. It’s also the home of Victoria Falls…the largest falls in the world.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "Tying the Knot at 13,000ft"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Tying the Knot at 13,000ft
programme.desc: A Romantic trip to Colorado takes a windy turn when Jason pushes Tayler, despite her hesitation, to summit Mt. Democrat in 70 mph winds and -19 degree weather.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "A Newly-Wed Recipe for Disaster"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 A Newly-Wed Recipe for Disaster
programme.desc: Jason loves mountain biking. Tayler prefers to read a book. Jason is up for any adventure tossed at him. Tayler likes to be a bit more prepared. Will the adventures that await in Arkansas leave the couple questioning if they have anything in common?

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "Theodora's Room"
programme.desc: Tayler and Jason travel to Eureka Springs, AR, a town with a rich history and home to one of the most haunted hotels in America, The Crescent. Jason convinces Tayler to stay one night, but will that stay lead to a night they don't want to remember?

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Gran Canaria"
programme.desc: Brothers Max & Benj Cave are challenged to create a run through an abandoned water park on the island of Gran Canaria.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Jamaica"
programme.desc: Brothers Max & Benj Cave are challenged to create a unique run in an unusal setting on the Island of Jamaica.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Best of ep. 7 to 11"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Best of ep. 7 to 11
programme.desc: In this episode the boys look back over their time spent in Portugal India Mexico Gran Canaria and Jamaica.

programme.title: Travel with a Goat S1 - "Goat"
programme.desc: Two foodies travel through Kenya with an animal. Will they decide to have the animal slaughtered after spending three days together with it?

programme.title: Travel with a Goat S1 - "Alpaca"
programme.desc: Two foodies travel through Peru with an animal. Will they decide to have the animal slaughtered after spending three days together with it?

programme.title: Travel with a Goat S1 - "Sheep"
programme.desc: Two foodies travel through Bulgaria with an animal. Will they decide to have the animal slaughtered after spending three days together with it?

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Lake Titicaca, Peru"
programme.desc: The brothers hitch-hike from the ancient Inca capitol of Cuzco, down the spine of the Andes Mountains to Lake Titicaca. They suffer acute mountain sickness, get high on coca leaves and meet some locals as they battle their way across the Andes.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing

programme.title: A Touch of Speed: The Art of Wing Chun
programme.desc: Uncover the principles of Wing Chun, a unique Chinese martial art renowned for its efficiency and directness. This program explores its legendary origins, core concepts like the center line theory, and its practical application in close-quarters combat. Witness the subtle power and intellectual depth of this sophisticated fighting system.

programme.title: A Touch of Speed: The Art of Wing Chun
programme.desc: Uncover the principles of Wing Chun, a unique Chinese martial art renowned for its efficiency and directness. This program explores its legendary origins, core concepts like the center line theory, and its practical application in close-quarters combat. Witness the subtle power and intellectual depth of this sophisticated fighting system.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving

programme.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programme.desc: The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and...

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius
programme.desc: "Foodwise" host Tianran He explores the ancient town of Qufu, the birthplace of Confucius. Together with a 75th generation descendant of the sage himself, they explore one of Confucius' lifelong pursuits: To cultivate oneself for the betterment of others. From the sophisticated cooking techniques and dining etiquette of Confucian cuisine, which was used for banqueting emperors and aristocrats, to getting hands on with street food and local delicacies, Tianran explores how Confucius' ideals have permeated through modern day life in Qufu.
